steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Triangle $QRS$ has a right angle at $R$. The side $SR = 47$ is the side opposite angle $Q$. Since the right angle is at $R$, the hypotenuse is $QS$. The condition $QR \lt QS$ confirms $QS$ is the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Apply the sine ratio from angle $Q$: $\sin Q = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \dfrac{SR}{QS} = \dfrac{47}{QS}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Solve for $QS$: multiply both sides by $QS$ and divide by $\sin Q$ to get $QS = \dfrac{47}{\sin Q}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• "$47\cos Q$": Cosine times a leg gives a shorter segment, not the hypotenuse; incorrect.
  • "$47\sin Q$": Multiplying by sine shrinks the value below 47, but $QS$ is the hypotenuse and must be the longest side; incorrect.
  • "$\frac{47}{\cos Q}$": this would follow from $\cos Q = 47/QS$, meaning 47 is the adjacent side; but 47 = SR is opposite to $Q$, not adjacent; incorrect.
  • "$\frac{47}{\sin Q}$": since $\sin Q = 47/QS$, rearranging gives $QS = 47/\sin Q$. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
